articles.title: Best Practices for Entering Overhead Costs

articles.gist: A wine's tax class determines how much excise tax is paid per gallon of wine as defined by the percentage of alcohol in the wine.

articles.title: Why gallons are showing in "Produced by Blending" in your 5120.17
articles.gist: If you are seeing gallons populate in the "Produced by Blending" or "Used for Blending" sections of your TTB 5120.17 report that you feel shouldn't be there, this may be the result of a tax class blend action.

articles.title: Activity Calendar
articles.gist: To access the Activity Calendar, select Calendar from the left-hand navigation menu.

articles.title: Harvest Calendar
articles.gist: To access the Harvest Calendar, select Calendar from the left-hand navigation menu.

articles.title: Winery Dashboard
articles.gist: The InnoVint Winery Dashboard enables winery members to understand the status of winery activity and inventory at a quick glance.

articles.title: Winery Search & Top Navigation Bar
articles.gist: The top navigation bar is always available at the top of your screen when you are logged into the InnoVint web platform.

articles.title: Depleting and Adjusting On Hand Dry Goods Inventory
articles.gist: Additive and packaging batches can be depleted within an Addition or Bottle action or task...

articles.title: How to Create and Receive Dry Goods - Additives & Packaging
articles.gist: InnoVint manages all of the Dry Goods and Liquid Solutions that you add to your wine from fruit to bottle.

articles.title: How to Import Dry Goods via CSV file
articles.gist: The Import Packaging action allows you to create new packaging products and batches, and receive new inventory via a CSV file.

articles.title: Navigating the Dry Goods Explorer & Details Pages
articles.gist: The Dry Goods Batch Tracking feature provides detailed tracking of your additive and packaging batch inventory.

articles.title: Reporting on Dry Goods
articles.gist: View the history of receipts, depletions or adjustments on each batch using the Dry Goods Explorer.

articles.title: Appellation Roll-up
articles.gist: Appellation Roll-up (or "AVA" Roll-up) is a helpful feature for winemakers to confirm label compliance.

articles.title: Calculated Additives
articles.gist: The Calculated Additives feature helps you trace and track additive amounts in your wine and juice lots—automatically.

articles.title: Changing Lot Properties
articles.gist: You can change the lot code, lot name, lot color or lot style at any point in time, whether your lot has contents or not, as long as your lot is not archived.

articles.title: How to Change Lot Stage
articles.gist: Lot stages can be changed at any point in time from the Lot Details page for a bulk wine, or for a bottled case good.

articles.title: How to Delete or Archive Lots and Vessels
articles.gist: Lots, Vessels, Dry Goods and Vineyard sources may be archived in InnoVint for numerous reasons.

articles.title: How to Import Lots via .csv File
articles.gist: The Import Lot action allows you to create multiple lots in bulk via a .csv file.

articles.title: Juice/Wine Lot Attributes
articles.gist: When creating a new juice or wine lot, users can designate the properties of that lot.

articles.title: Lot Details Page
articles.gist: The Lot Details Page is the home base for all details regarding a specific lot and there is a huge amount of data to explore.

articles.title: Lot Explorer
articles.gist: The Lot Explorer is your window to your juice/wine lot (bulk wine) inventory, giving you control and flexibility when viewing and managing your lots.

articles.title: Bond to Bond Transfers (B2B)
articles.gist: InnoVint provides different options for receiving or transferring inventory into or out of bond.

articles.title: Bond to Bond Transfers (B2B) within your InnoVint Winery
articles.gist: If you would like to transfer wine to another winery in a separate InnoVint account, or outside of InnoVint, please check out this article.

articles.title: How to Record a Blend
articles.gist: The Blend action is used for combining two or more lots together to create a new wine lot or a new composition of an existing lot.

articles.title: How to Record a Bottling
articles.gist: When you're ready to bottle your wines, InnoVint helps you to easily record the details of your bottling into the system.

articles.title: How to Record a Rack
articles.gist: The Rack action can be performed:.

articles.title: How to Record a Rack and Return
articles.gist: Note: The Rack and Return action does not allow multiple lots to be blended then returned to the same vessels.

articles.title: How to Record a Transfer
articles.gist: The Transfer feature allows you to move volume between lots and vessels and can be performed:.

articles.title: How to Record Filtration (the Filter Action)
articles.gist: Any time you are running your wine through filtration and want to track this activity, use the Filter action or task.

articles.title: How to print Lot Labels
articles.gist: Lot labels can be printed in bulk for many lots at once or one lot at a time.

articles.title: How to print QR Code Vessel Labels
articles.gist: QR codes can be printed in bulk for many vessels at once or one at a time.

articles.title: Troubleshooting printer settings for labels
articles.gist: Q: My labels are not aligning correctly when I print from the PDF.

articles.title: Which labels are recommended and where you can purchase them
articles.gist: We strongly recommend purchasing weatherproof labels.

articles.title: Attributing an action to a specific individual
articles.gist: Only work orders, not actions, can be attributed to a specific person on the team by assigning it to them with a task.

articles.title: Creating a New Lot within an Action or Task
articles.gist: You can create a new lot via the +Add lot button on the Lot Explorer.

articles.title: How to backdate an action
articles.gist: Backdating is an available feature in direct actions and work orders.

articles.title: How to Edit or Delete Recorded Actions
articles.gist: Direct actions immediately record a movement or piece of data to InnoVint when they are submitted.

articles.title: How to set the contents of a vessel during a lot movement
articles.gist: When completing a movement in an action or work order task, you need to specify the change of contents on each vessel involved in the action.

articles.title: Using a Custom Action or Custom Task
articles.gist: A Custom Action/Task is a flexible, editable action that can be applied to the entire winery, a single lot, or one or more vessels.

articles.title: Volume Adjustments
articles.gist: The volume adjustment action can be used to remedy errors in data entry, to account for spillage and inventory losses, for initial onboarding of lots into InnoVint, and other miscellaneous reasons in accordance with proper TTB reporting.

articles.title: Bottling Report
articles.gist: The Bottling report lives under the Reporting tab in the left navigation bar.

articles.title: Custom Reports
articles.gist: The future of InnoVint reporting is here!

articles.title: Lot Properties History Report
articles.gist: The Lot Properties History Report gives you a winery-level view of all lot property and attribute changes over time.

articles.title: Report Explorer
articles.gist: To access the Report Explorer, select the icon from the left-hand navigation menu.

articles.title: Unlock the Power of Custom Reports: Example Videos
articles.gist: Looking for some inspiration to help you get more out of our Custom Reports feature?

articles.title: Winery Activity Feed
articles.gist: The Winery Activity Feed displays all of the activities recorded in InnoVint, by date, across all of your inventory.

articles.title: Adding, Editing, or Removing Tags
articles.gist: Tags are simple to add and remove, and can be edited at any time.

articles.title: How to add Photos & File Attachments in Notes
articles.gist: Want to attach a BOL to a bond transfer in, or take a picture of the fruit quality when receiving fruit?

articles.title: How to edit or delete a note
articles.gist: Recorded notes on lots, vessels, dry goods, vineyards, and actions can be edited or deleted by the person who created the note.

articles.title: What Tags are used for in InnoVint
articles.gist: InnoVint records and stores a lot of data pertaining to the specifics of your lots, vessels, additives, and vineyard sources.

articles.title: Fill level change in topped vessels
articles.gist: The volume of the vessel(s) being topped via a Topping action/task will NOT increase.

articles.title: How to Record a Top Off
articles.gist: About the Top Off feature.

articles.title: How to record Topping
articles.gist: About the Topping feature.

articles.title: How Topping wines are blended into topped lots
articles.gist: The components of the topping wine are blended proportionally across the lot(s) being topped.

articles.title: Change or Edit Vessel Details
articles.gist: After creation of a vessel, if you find that one or more of the vessel characteristics are incorrect, have changed, or need to be updated, you can edit the vessel details to correct the vessel information.

articles.title: How to Import Vessels via .csv file
articles.gist: The Import Vessels action lets you upload multiple new vessels at once using a .csv file.

articles.title: Managing and Using Vessel Locations
articles.gist: This feature allows users to add location information to any vessel.

articles.title: Vessel Details Page
articles.gist: Navigate to the Vessel Details page by selecting a vessel from the Vessel Explorer.

articles.title: How to Archive a Vineyard or Vineyard Block
articles.gist: If one of your vineyards or a vineyard block is no longer in use, you can archive it to remove it from view.

articles.title: How to track vineyard data in Fruit lots
articles.gist: This article covers how to track vineyard data in accounts without the GROW module (formerly known as Advanced Vineyard Tracking).

articles.title: Creating Work Order Templates
articles.gist: Work order templating is a great tool to save frequently-assigned work that contains detailed instruction or multiple tasks.

articles.title: Deleting a Work Order
articles.gist: Work orders can be deleted at any time before they are submitted, but can only be deleted from the web interface/desktop app, and not from the mobile app.

articles.title: How to Create Recurring Work Orders
articles.gist: The Recurring Work Order feature allows you to create work orders to recur in the future at different intervals of your choosing.

articles.title: How to Edit a Work Order
articles.gist: Open work orders can be edited to change the title, due date, and assigned to, as well as instructions, and vessel & lot selections within the tasks.

articles.title: Notifications in InnoVint
articles.gist: Notifications are enabled in InnoVint's MAKE desktop functionality as helpful reminders of important work order activity.

articles.title: Printing Work Orders
articles.gist: Learn about our available Work Order print options, how they differ, and how to optimize your print settings.

articles.title: Skipping a Task within a Work Order
articles.gist: After a work order has been created, the assignee can choose not to complete one or more of the tasks within the work order by "skipping" them.

articles.title: Using Work Orders in InnoVint
articles.gist: Creating work orders in InnoVint allows you to plan and schedule winery tasks to be assigned and distributed to your team for completion.

articles.title: Custom Lot Attributes
articles.gist: This is available with the MAKE-Plus subscription.

articles.title: Blend Trials
articles.gist: The Blend Trials feature is a way for users to mock up potential blends in the winery to see if they meet composition, production, cost, analysis and taste targets.

articles.title: Tank Maps
articles.gist: The Tank Map feature allows users to build a digital replica of their winery's tank map.

articles.title: InnoVint + Baker Wine & Grape Analysis Integration
articles.gist: Baker Wine & Grape Analysis has coordinated with InnoVint to build an integration that allows for your analysis results to be posted directly into InnoVint.

articles.title: InnoVint + Barrelwise Integration
articles.gist: Setting up the integration.

articles.title: InnoVint + ETS Integration Overview
articles.gist: InnoVint and ETS have established a partnership that allows for analysis results to be posted directly into InnoVint's platform.

articles.title: InnoVint + Onafis Integration
articles.gist: Onafis and InnoVint have partnered to enable two-way data synchronization between your InnoVint winery account and your Onafis densimeters.

articles.title: InnoVint + TankNET Integration
articles.gist: When the TankNET integration with InnoVint is activated, InnoVint will pull data from your TankNET server(s) on a regular schedule and record as analysis in your account, saving you time and energy - especially during harvest!

articles.title: InnoVint + VinWizard Integration
articles.gist: If you have mapped all your vessels, and see the Failure Reason "Vessel not assigned to any lot," that just means your tanks are empty in InnoVint.

articles.title: InnoVint + WinePulse Integration: Measuring Profitability
articles.gist: Both Cost Tracking and Case Goods modules are required to utilize the InnoVint + WinePulse Integration.

articles.title: Intended Use Overview
articles.gist: Intended Use is currently a "beta" feature, available to selected subscription levels.

articles.title: Owner-based Permissions and Member Capabilities (Overview/Highlight)
articles.gist: Activating owner-based permissions in InnoVint allows your winery to track inventory by Owner and control user accessibility by granting them access to one or more Owners in the account.

articles.title: Owner-based Permissions System (Highlight Video)
articles.gist: For more information on InnoVint's Owner-based Permissions System, please see this article in the Support Center.

articles.title: Setting up your Custom Crush Permissions
articles.gist: You have just activated a permission-based system and you're ready to get started setting up your account!

articles.title: Bottling en Tirage
articles.gist: Bottling en Tirage is a special movement that allows you to move your sparkling base wine into bottles for the secondary fermentation, and track those bottles within tirage bins in your bulk inventory (Part 1, Section A of the TTB Report).

articles.title: Disgorge, Dosage & Packaging
articles.gist: InnoVint supports a standalone Disgorge, Dosage and Package action for our Sparkling module users.

articles.title: Riddling
articles.gist: Select the Riddling action.

articles.title: Sparkling Wine Module Overview
articles.gist: InnoVint's Sparkling module allows you to track all steps of the Traditional Method sparkling wine production process.

articles.title: Sparkling Wine Module Workflows
articles.gist: This feature requires activation.

articles.title: Accessing Your InnoVint Account
articles.gist: InnoVint is committed to ensuring that your data remains safe and secure.

articles.title: How to change or reset your password
articles.gist: Note: Passwords can only be changed or reset from the web interface (cellar.innovint.us).

articles.title: InnoVint iOS Work Order app: iPhone & iPod Touch
articles.gist: The original InnoVint iOS Work Order app is no longer available or supported.

articles.title: InnoVint on iPad or tablet
articles.gist: We recommend saving the InnoVint desktop browser app (the one you would normally use on your desktop computer) to your tablet home screen the first time you open it.

articles.title: Log into InnoVint on a Laptop or Desktop
articles.gist: For InnoVint's MAKE, GROW or FINANCE products.

articles.title: Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) Settings
articles.gist: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is now an account security option that you opt into at the "user" level.

articles.title: Password Guidelines
articles.gist: A little while ago, we updated our password policy and we want to take the time to explain the changes, as well as our motivation.

articles.title: Session Timeouts
articles.gist: Session timeouts are live, starting on July 30 2024!

articles.title: Which application do I use?
articles.gist: InnoVint has one app for mobile devices, as well as our "desktop" browser app (the one you use on your computer) to support MAKE, GROW and FINANCE.

articles.title: An Introduction to InnoVint's Support Center
articles.gist: Welcome to the Support Center!

articles.title: How to Access the Support Center and Submit a Help Ticket
articles.gist: To access the Support Center through InnoVint's desktop app, click the question mark icon in the upper right hand corner of your screen.

articles.title: Getting Started Checklist for MAKE ✅
articles.gist: Getting your winery up and running with MAKE is a straight-forward process.

articles.title: How to Onboard Inventory - Overview
articles.gist: Have you reached Step Two of the Getting Started Checklist?

articles.title: New User to InnoVint
articles.gist: We are excited to welcome you to InnoVint and want to give you some resources to be able to dive straight into your winery account.

articles.title: Pre-Onboarding Guide & Checklist!
articles.gist: Your onboarding date is set and we are excited to get you started!

articles.title: Step 1: Add Vineyard Sources
articles.gist: Welcome to InnoVint! If at any time you need assistance, please submit a help ticket right here in the Support Center (link in the top right corner) or email support@innovint.us.

articles.title: Step 2: Add your vessels
articles.gist: Every juice/wine lot created in InnoVint needs to be filled into vessels.

articles.title: Step 3: Add your lots
articles.gist: To begin, go to the Lot Explorer by clicking on Lots in the left navigation bar.

articles.title: Step 4: Fill lots with volume
articles.gist: It's now time to add the contents to your lots.

articles.title: Display Preferences in Settings
articles.gist: What can you set in Display Preferences?

articles.title: How to Add a New Bond in MAKE
articles.gist: Need to add a new bond to your InnoVint MAKE account?

articles.title: Managing and Using Dip Charts in InnoVint
articles.gist: Dip charts are not available at all subscription levels.

articles.title: Managing and Using Shipping Locations in InnoVint
articles.gist: Destination locations can be saved to your InnoVint account to help you quickly fill in the 'Shipped to' details within a Bill of Lading.

articles.title: Member Management: How to Add, Edit or Remove Users
articles.gist: Do you want to grant access to InnoVint for your cellar crew and winery staff?

articles.title: Overview: User Permissions and Capability Levels
articles.gist: Winery members and users can be granted 1 of 4 different capability levels.

articles.title: Winery Lock Backdating
articles.gist: The Lock Backdating capability allows Admin users to set the earliest date and time at which actions can be backdated in InnoVint.

articles.title: How to Add Inventory
articles.gist: The Add Inventory action allows you to add inventory to one or multiple SKUs and can be accessed:.

articles.title: How to Edit or Delete Inventory Actions
articles.gist: All submitted inventory actions can easily be edited and deleted using the “Edit Action” and “Delete Action” buttons in the top right corner.

articles.title: How to Import Depletions in SUPPLY
articles.gist: SUPPLY streamlines workflows while maintaining accurate inventory.

articles.title: How to Move or Deplete Inventory
articles.gist: The Move and Deplete inventory actions allow you to easily update your current inventory across locations.

articles.title: How to Reconcile Inventory
articles.gist: Taking inventory? The Reconcile Inventory action allows you to true up your inventory after doing a physical count.

articles.title: How to use "On order" Status in SUPPLY
articles.gist: "On order" status in SUPPLY helps you more easily understand which inventory is attributed to an unfulfilled order and is not currently available to sell.

articles.title: How to Add Bonds and Locations in SUPPLY
articles.gist: Both bonds and locations must be added on the backend by InnoVint’s Support Team.

articles.title: How to Add SKUs
articles.gist: SUPPLY is the source of truth for your case goods inventory.

articles.title: How to Onboard Inventory in SUPPLY
articles.gist: The Onboard inventory action is used when onboarding into SUPPLY and bringing in inventory that was previously tracked somewhere else.

articles.title: Navigating SUPPLY
articles.gist: The top and left-hand navigation bars are always available on the edges of your screen when you are logged into the SUPPLY web platform.

articles.title: SKU Details Page
articles.gist: The SKU Details Page is the home base for all details regarding a specific SKU.

articles.title: SKU Explorer
articles.gist: The SKU Explorer is the homepage for SUPPLY.

articles.title: The SUPPLY Onboarding Checklist
articles.gist: Getting your case goods inventory management up and running with InnoVint is a straight-forward process.

articles.title: Adding and Using Notes in SUPPLY
articles.gist: You can now add a Note to any action in SUPPLY to record important details — such as bottling information, reasons for depletions, or invoice numbers — directly within the action itself.

articles.title: Compliance Reporting: How does SUPPLY populate the TTB Report?
articles.gist: SUPPLY provides a comprehensive export to help you populate Section B - Bottled Wines for the TTB Report 5120.17.

articles.title: How to Archive or Delete SKUs
articles.gist: This article explains how to archive, unarchive, and delete SKUs in SUPPLY.

articles.title: How to create a Bill of Lading (BOL) in SUPPLY
articles.gist: Create and print a Bill of Lading document from within SUPPLY using your SKU inventory data and information tracked within the platform.

articles.title: Reporting in SUPPLY
articles.gist: This article outlines the different reporting options that exist in SUPPLY.

articles.title: SUPPLY + Commerce7 Integration
articles.gist: InnoVint's SUPPLY product aims to provide a single source of truth for your case goods inventory.

articles.title: SUPPLY FAQ
articles.gist: There are some common questions that come up when you are exploring SUPPLY.

articles.title: Tracking Case Goods - MAKE to SUPPLY
articles.gist: When it comes to tracking movements of cased goods in and out of bond, SUPPLY is intended to be the final source of truth for case goods inventory management, streamlining inventory control and compliance across multiple locations.

articles.title: Does InnoVint take product requests?
articles.gist: At InnoVint, we appreciate the diversity of approaches within the winery community, recognizing that each winery has its unique style, workflows, and preferences for data presentation.

articles.title: Support hours and FAQ
articles.gist: Our Support Hours are Monday to Friday 8am - 5pm Pacific Time.
